_format: Exemption file for tools/sweep-preflight-scanner.py. Each entry suppresses a single (path, pattern_id, line_hash) false-positive that the in-scanner heuristics do not catch. line_hash is the first 16 hex chars of SHA-256(stripped_line_content); if the line text changes, the hash no longer matches and the candidate re-surfaces (which is the correct behaviour: the maintainer re-triages the changed content).
_maintenance: Add an entry after the validation-sweep history register dismisses a finding as a stable false positive. Remove an entry if the underlying text is rewritten to remove the false-positive shape. The register's false-positive memory section is the human-readable source of truth; this file is the machine-readable mirror.

exemptions.reason: addyosmani external vet count: '5 skills in full + 18 spot-scanned' describes the maintainer's vetting depth, not a pack-skills count.

addyosmani external description: '24 engineering-workflow skills in Claude Code's Skills format'. The scanner captures a smaller digit from later in the line; the prose is about an external project, not the pack.
Synthesis rubric sub-rules: 'Four rules, no ceremony' refers to rubric sub-rules 5.1-5.4, not governance-rule count.
promptmap2 external project: '69 rules across 6 directories' describes that external scanner's ruleset, not the pack.
promptmap2 external project (second mention on same page): '69 rules across 6 categories' describes that external scanner's ruleset.

Sweep 5 entry meta-quote: the register narrates that the prior sweep dismissed 'Four rules, no ceremony' as a synthesis-rubric sub-rule reference. Quoting that false positive does not make it a current stale claim; the underlying false positive is already exempted at SKILL.md:89.

SARIF-lite output format introduction: the line is 'Three rules:' introducing the three rules of the SARIF-lite output format (one block per finding, deterministic fingerprint, closed severity enum), not a governance-rule count.
